questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a constituent particle of an atom?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Photon**

**Explanation:**
Atoms are made of three main subatomic particles: electrons, protons, and neutrons. Electrons revolve around the nucleus while protons and neutrons are present inside the nucleus.

**Option-wise analysis:**
Electron – Negatively charged particle discovered by J.J. Thomson. It exists outside the nucleus.
Proton – Positively charged particle discovered by Rutherford.
Neutron – Neutral particle discovered by James Chadwick.
Photon – A photon is a quantum of electromagnetic radiation (light particle). It is NOT a component of an atom.

**Exam extension:**
• Electron charge = −1.6×10⁻¹⁹ C
• Proton charge = +1.6×10⁻¹⁹ C
• Neutron charge = 0

questions.text: A single type of atom is found in which of the following?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Native elements**

**Explanation:**
Native elements are elements that occur in nature in their pure form consisting of only one type of atom.

**Option-wise analysis:**
Compounds – Contain two or more elements chemically combined.
Mixtures – Contain different substances physically mixed.
Native elements – Only one type of atom (example: gold, silver).
Alloys – Mixtures of metals.

**Exam extension:**
Examples of native elements: Gold (Au), Silver (Ag), Copper (Cu).

questions.text: Which particles constitute the nucleus of an atom?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Proton and neutron**

**Explanation:**
The atomic nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons are present outside the nucleus in orbitals.

**Option-wise analysis:**
Electron and proton – Electron is outside the nucleus.
Electron and neutron – Electrons are not in nucleus.
Proton and neutron – Correct combination forming the nucleus.
Proton, neutron and electron – Electron is not present inside nucleus.

**Exam extension:**
• Proton number = Atomic number
• Mass number = Protons + Neutrons

questions.text: Which statement correctly describes the structure of an atom?
options: Neutrons and electrons inside nucleus, protons revolve
Electrons and protons inside nucleus, neutrons revolve
Protons and neutrons inside nucleus, electrons revolve
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Protons and neutrons inside nucleus, electrons revolve**

**Explanation:**
The nucleus contains protons and neutrons while electrons revolve around the nucleus in energy levels according to the Bohr model.

**Exam extension:**
• Bohr model explains electron shells (K, L, M, N).
• Modern quantum mechanics uses orbitals instead of fixed orbits.

questions.text: Who discovered the atomic nucleus?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Rutherford**

**Explanation:**
Ernest Rutherford discovered the nucleus through the famous gold foil experiment (1911).

**Option-wise analysis:**
Dalton – Proposed atomic theory.
Einstein – Developed theory of relativity.
Thomson – Discovered electron.

**Exam extension:**
Gold foil experiment showed most of the atom is empty space.

questions.text: Who discovered the neutron?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: James Chadwick (1932)**

**Explanation:**
Chadwick discovered the neutron by bombarding beryllium with alpha particles.

**Exam extension:**
Neutron mass ≈ proton mass.
Neutron has no charge.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a fundamental subatomic particle?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Deuteron**

**Explanation:**
A deuteron is the nucleus of deuterium consisting of one proton and one neutron, not a fundamental particle.

**Exam extension:**
Hydrogen isotopes:
• Protium
• Deuterium
• Tritium

questions.text: Which pair represents a particle and its antiparticle?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Electron – Positron**

**Explanation:**
Positron is the antiparticle of electron. It has the same mass but opposite charge.

**Exam extension:**
Antimatter concept introduced by Dirac.

questions.text: An alpha particle has approximately the same mass as:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Helium atom**

**Explanation:**
Alpha particle = Helium nucleus (2 protons + 2 neutrons).

**Exam extension:**
Alpha particles are emitted in radioactive decay.

questions.text: The nucleus of helium contains:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: 2 protons and 2 neutrons**

**Explanation:**
Helium-4 nucleus consists of two protons and two neutrons.

questions.text: Which of the following carries a negative charge?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Beta particles**

**Explanation:**
Beta particles are high-speed electrons emitted during radioactive decay.

questions.text: The order of filling electrons in orbitals is governed by:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Aufbau principle**

**Explanation:**
Electrons fill orbitals in order of increasing energy.

questions.text: Magnetic quantum number indicates:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Orientation of orbital**

**Explanation:**
Magnetic quantum number determines spatial orientation of orbitals.

questions.text: An atom contains 2 protons and 2 neutrons. What is its mass number?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: 4**

Mass number = protons + neutrons = 2 + 2 = 4.

questions.text: Atoms with the same number of protons but different number of neutrons are called:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Isotopes**

Example: Carbon-12, Carbon-14.

questions.text: Radioactivity was discovered by:
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Becquerel (1896)**

He discovered natural radioactivity in uranium salts.

questions.text: Isotopes have:
options: Same mass number different atomic number
Different mass number same atomic number
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Different mass number but same atomic number**

questions.text: Isotopes are nuclei having:
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Same protons different neutrons**

questions.text: Which of the following represents an isoneutronic group?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: C14, N15, O16**

They have the same number of neutrons.

questions.text: Radioactivity is measured using which instrument?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Geiger Counter**

Used to detect and measure ionizing radiation.
